description: P-value boundary rounding — the log-rank p-value is 0.0495 but rounded to 0.05. This crosses the conventional significance boundary (0.05) and could change the study conclusion.
severity_rationale: Per the error taxonomy (Class A), an error that changes the analysis conclusion or regulatory interpretation is critical. Rounding 0.0495 -> 0.05 flips the primary endpoint from statistically significant (p<0.05) to non-significant, which can change the regulatory decision. Severity corrected from 'minor' to 'critical' in QC review.

TC-003.note: NOTE: chi-square 3.857 on 1 df gives p=0.0495 (true). Agent rounded to 0.05. This changes interpretation from significant (p<0.05) to non-significant (p=0.05). [chi-square corrected from 3.85 -> 3.857 in QC review so it is numerically consistent with the stated true p-value of 0.0495.]

detection_expectation.detectable_rationale: Recomputing the p-value from the reported chi-square (3.857, 1 df -> 0.0495) and comparing against the reported 0.05 makes this detectable by an agent that cross-checks statistics rather than trusting the rounded display. Changed from false to true in QC review.
detection_expectation.rule_violated: ICH E9: p-values should be reported with sufficient precision (typically 4 decimal places)
detection_expectation.correct_action: Display p-value as 0.0495 or 0.049, not rounded to 0.05. Flag boundary-rounding if detected.

metadata.notes: Subtle error that changes study interpretation. Hard to detect without cross-checking raw chi-square value.
